functions.description: Allocates the next 0x38-byte object from the shared 7000-entry fringe-manager pool, or raises the `Too many Fringes` error if the pool overflows. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRFringeManager::GetFringe()`.

functions.name: uninit_nuke
functions.description: Kills the 25 sprite slots owned by the exact 0x7c-byte cRNuke collision-ring effect at Player +0x150 and clears its active state. Android and iOS retain this method as `cRNuke::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_nuke
functions.description: Initializes the exact 0x7c-byte cRNuke owner at Player +0x150: spawns the 25-sprite ring effect used by collision kinds `2` and `6`, seeds its orbit height and phase step from the owning player and global tick delta, and immediately dispatches `cRNuke::AI()`. Android retains this method as `cRNuke::Init()`.

functions.name: update_nuke
functions.description: Advances the exact cRNuke owner by moving its 25 sprite slots around the containing player at radius `7.0`, using the +0x08/+0x0c z-step pair and +0x10/+0x14 phase pair. Android and iOS retain this method as `cRNuke::AI()`.

functions.name: recycle_bod_to_free_list
functions.description: Exact emitted `cLinkedList<cRBod>::Remove` helper: unlinks one active BOD, moves it onto the shared free-list head, and diagnoses invalid ownership or active-iteration removal. The same owned `BodList` body is inlined by pickup AI teardown paths.

functions.name: copy_segment_definition_to_level_slot
functions.description: Implements the void `cRSubTracks::ImportSegment(char*, cRSubSegment*)` member: finds a root-owned catalog entry by its enumerated filename, transposes its column-major eight-lane glyph grid into a caller-selected SubSegment, and copies the authored row metadata including position and velocity vectors, path template, and ring-speed fields. The Windows body does not read its receiver, but all three callers establish the SubTracks owner in ECX before passing an ordinary, First, or Last 0x4220-byte inline destination.

functions.name: load_level_definition_file
functions.description: Parses one Levels/*.txt definition into the owning SubTracks instance, including its SubSegments, display metadata, speed, hazard frequencies, landscape, parcel, and texture fields. Arcade definitions also populate the selected GalaxyRouteSlot's detail and description text in the root-owned Galaxy controller. Symbol-preserving iOS builds name the corresponding void overload `cRSubTracks::Init(char*)`; all Windows callers discard the path-dependent incidental value left in EAX.

functions.name: load_builtin_segment_definitions
functions.description: Initializes the secondary SubTracks owner from the shipped `SubSegmentRaw*` table at `0x4a63d0`, including records such as `Start`, `Finish`, and `Filler` plus their eight authored glyph rows and path metadata. Every live 0x48-byte raw record also owns a distinct `marker_row` pointer at +0x24 whose width matches the glyph rows and whose text contains one `*`; Windows does not consume that lane or the preceding six metadata dwords here. The inconsistent incidental return register proves the member is void; symbol-preserving iOS builds name the corresponding overload `cRSubTracks::Init(cRSubSegmentRaw**)`.

functions.name: load_segment_definitions
functions.description: Implements the void `cRSMTracks::Import()` member: parses SEGMENTS/*.TXT into the exact 0x25cfb4-byte SMTracks owner, stores the internal `Name:'...'` display label separately from the enumerated filename, and fills each 0x4088-byte entry's complete 256-row authored metadata bank with parcel, model, velocity, path, ring, ring-speed, and jetpack-off data.

functions.name: load_level_definitions
functions.description: Implements the void `cRSMTracks::OpenLevels()` member by enumerating LEVELS/*.TXT and parsing each name into the root-owned SubTracks scratch definition.

functions.name: format_time_trial_string
functions.description: Implements `cRTimeTrial::TimeString(cRTime&)`: formats one authored Time/cRTime value into the shared Time Trial HUD buffer. The receiver is the exact 0x330-byte TimeTrial owner embedded at SubgameRuntime +0xff25e0, although the body does not read receiver fields.

functions.name: kill_tip_widgets
functions.description: Tip-owned helper that kills the live border widgets for one cRTip, including the main body and any optional OK or Disable buttons, then clears Tip::active.

functions.name: initialize_tip
functions.description: Implements `cRTip::Init(cRTipData*, ...)`: builds one Tip-owned main border widget, optional OK or Disable buttons, and the timed auto-dismiss rate for transient tips. The Windows hide-disable-button formal is a 32-bit int even though mobile ports spell it bool or signed char.

functions.name: update_tip
functions.description: Exact `cRTip::AI()` member: runs one Tip, handling hide or show state, button-click consumption, timed expiry, and the final teardown or frontend-state restore path.

functions.name: initialize_tip_manager
functions.description: Exact `cRTipManager::Init()` member that clears the three inline Tips at TipManager +0x38.

functions.name: uninit_tips
functions.description: Exact `cRTipManager::UnInitTips()` member that tears down widgets for each active inline Tip.

functions.name: enqueue_tip_message
functions.description: Exact `cRTipManager::TipNew(cRTipData*, ...)` member: selects one of the three inline Tips, initializes it from authored TipData, returns the selected Tip, and warns if all three are active.

functions.name: update_tip_manager
functions.description: Exact `cRTipManager::AI()` member that advances each active inline Tip.

functions.name: initialize_tutorial
functions.description: Exact Windows `Tutorial::initialize_tutorial`: seeds the embedded 0x1c-byte tutorial owner, borrows its containing SubgameRuntime, and applies the tutorial runtime flags. Android and iOS retain `cRTutorial::Init()`.

functions.name: uninit_tutorial
functions.description: Exact Windows `Tutorial::uninit_tutorial` thunk that tears down the root tip manager for tutorial-mode exit. Android and iOS retain `cRTutorial::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_tutorial
functions.description: Exact Windows `Tutorial::update_tutorial` thunk: samples the live Goldy position through the borrowed containing SubgameRuntime. Android and iOS retain `cRTutorial::AI()`.

functions.name: reset_voice_manager
functions.description: Exact `cRVoiceManager::ReSet()` member: clears global voice progress and all 16 inline VoiceSet cooldowns.

functions.name: initialize_voice_manager
functions.description: Recovered `cRVoiceManager::Init()` member: parses the authored 16-set Voice/_Voice.txt bank, registers each sample, applies normalization and frequency settings, and resets cooldown state.

functions.name: update_voice_manager
functions.description: Exact `cRVoiceManager::AI()` member: advances all 16 inline VoiceSets and the manager's global voice-frequency timer.

functions.name: play_voice_manager
functions.description: Exact `cRVoiceManager::Play(int, int, int)` member: applies current-voice and frequency gating, optionally stops the active sample, and dispatches through the selected inline VoiceSet.

functions.name: initialize_bass_audio_backend
functions.description: Extracts Bass.dll from game data into tBass.dll, loads the BASS 2.0 exports dynamically, and initializes the audio backend.

functions.name: uninitialize_bass_audio_backend
functions.description: Stops the global BASS backend, frees the loaded `Bass.dll` state, unloads the temporary DLL, and deletes the extracted `tBass.dll` copy.

functions.name: ensure_music_stream_from_path
functions.description: Ensures the requested music path is the active BASS stream, skipping a restart when the cached path already matches and otherwise stopping any previous stream before opening and playing the new path.

functions.name: prepare_music_stream_reload_if_path_changed
functions.description: Checks whether the requested music path differs from the cached active path and, when it does, stops the current music stream so `cache_music_file` can reload it from archive bytes.

functions.name: play_music_stream_from_bytes
functions.description: Stops any previous active music stream, opens a new BASS stream from already-read music bytes, and starts playback while caching the source path.

functions.name: stop_music_stream
functions.description: Stops the active BASS music stream, removes any registered sync callback, frees the stream handle, and clears the active-stream flag.

functions.name: load_registered_sound_sample_from_path
functions.description: Loads one sample directly from a filesystem path into the shared runtime sound-bank slot table via `BASS_SampleLoad(mem=0, ...)`, storing the resulting sample handle at the requested registered-sample index.

functions.name: load_registered_sound_sample_from_bytes
functions.description: Loads one sample from already-read archive bytes into the shared runtime sound-bank slot table via `BASS_SampleLoad(mem=1, ...)`, storing the resulting sample handle at the requested registered-sample index.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_scaled
functions.description: AudioBackend method that plays one registered runtime sample through BASS with the supplied normalized gain scale and the default pitch and pan parameters.

functions.name: stop_sound_sample_handle
functions.description: Forwards one raw runtime sample handle directly into `BASS_SampleStop`, underneath the registered-sample and warning-owned stop wrappers.

functions.name: stop_registered_sound_sample
functions.description: AudioBackend method that stops one registered runtime sample index by resolving it through the shared sound-bank table before calling BASS.

functions.name: is_registered_sound_sample_playing
functions.description: AudioBackend method that reports whether one registered runtime sample index is active by querying its shared sound-bank table entry through BASS.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_default
functions.description: Plays one registered runtime sample index through the default `BASS_SamplePlayEx` wrapper with the hard-coded playback parameters used by the warning-owned sample path.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_backend
functions.description: AudioBackend method that forwards one registered runtime sample index plus normalized volume and pitch into the `BASS_SamplePlayEx` lane shared by the SFX and voice wrappers.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_scaled_panned
functions.description: AudioBackend method that plays one registered runtime sample through BASS with normalized volume plus explicit pitch and pan overrides.

functions.name: set_global_sample_volume_config
functions.description: Scales one normalized [0,1] sample-volume scalar onto BASS config option `4` (`BASS_CONFIG_GVOL_SAMPLE`) before the backend stores it as the live global SFX sample volume.

functions.name: set_global_stream_volume_config
functions.description: Scales one normalized [0,1] stream-volume scalar onto BASS config option `5` (`BASS_CONFIG_GVOL_STREAM`) before the backend stores it as the live music or stream volume.

functions.name: stop_audio_backend
functions.description: Forwards directly into the global BASS stop export to halt active backend playback during shutdown or full audio resets.

functions.name: resume_audio_backend_if_paused
functions.description: Restarts the global BASS backend only when the audio state flag at `backend + 0x18` says the backend is currently paused, then clears that paused flag.

functions.name: pause_audio_backend_if_running
functions.description: Pauses the global BASS backend only when the audio state flag at `backend + 0x18` says the backend is currently running, then marks it paused.

functions.name: set_audio_normalization_scales
functions.description: Stores the normalized music, SFX, and voice gain scales parsed from `VOICE/_VOICE.TXT` into the shared backend lanes that the music, sound, and voice wrappers multiply into live playback volume.

functions.name: debug_report_stub
functions.description: Stripped release-build variadic debug reporter; it ignores every caller-owned argument and returns zero.

functions.name: initialize_global_font3d_bods_thunk
functions.description: CRT constructor-table thunk that tail-forwards to the fixed-array initializer for g_font3d_bods.

functions.name: initialize_global_font3d_bods
functions.description: Runs the BodBase constructor over all 128 entries of the global 3D-font BOD pool at a 0x38-byte stride.

functions.name: initialize_global_font_queue_colors_thunk
functions.description: CRT constructor-table thunk that tail-forwards to the cFontPrintBuffer color-subobject initializer.

functions.name: initialize_global_font_queue_colors
functions.description: Runs the folded trivial tColour constructor over the +0x6c color member of all 1024 global cFontPrintBuffer entries at a 0x84-byte stride. Android independently preserves the authored cFontPrintBuffer class name, identical stride, and color-member offset.

functions.name: initialize_font_wave_state
functions.description: Seeds the shared sine-wave text animation phases and per-tick phase increments that `draw_font_text_instance` consumes for wobble text.

functions.name: update_font_wave_state
functions.description: Void FontAI counterpart: advances the shared sine-wave text animation phases, wraps them into `[0, 2pi)`, and resets the transient font-wave bookkeeping lanes each frame. Its sole Windows caller discards EAX.

functions.name: font_slot_index_for_char
functions.description: Maps front-end text bytes onto FONT-MENU-HOVER atlas slot indices; shipped tutorial strings reuse bytes like `{`, `}`, `[`, `]`, `~`, `_`, `^`, and `;` as icon tokens.

functions.name: measure_font_text_width
functions.description: Accumulates atlas slot widths for one string using the registered font's spacing scalar and trailing space padding.

functions.name: register_font_texture_sheet
functions.description: Loads one FONT-MENU-HOVER atlas, scans row-0 white delimiters into resettable glyph runs and the column-0 height marker, registers single- or split-page texture refs with their source-retention policy, and stores per-font UV, page, and scale metadata.

functions.name: draw_font_text_instance
functions.description: Void FontPrintReal counterpart over one cFontPrintBuffer: maps each byte through the atlas slot table and submits textured glyph quads. The queue drain discards EAX on every path.

functions.name: draw_queued_font_quad_instance
functions.description: Void OSDPrintReal counterpart over one cFontPrintBuffer: forwards its stored coordinates, UVs, size mode, angle, and color into the shared immediate textured-quad helper. The queue drain discards EAX.

functions.name: draw_font_text_queue
functions.description: Void FontPrintRender counterpart: iterates the live cFontPrintBuffer queue backward and draws entries matching the supplied render mask. Its sole Windows caller discards EAX.

functions.name: queue_font_text_instance
functions.description: FontPrint counterpart: copies one text run into the shared font print string buffer and appends the matching cFontPrintBuffer record with its font, position, alignment, colors, and render flags.

functions.name: queue_axis_aligned_textured_quad
functions.description: Appends one axis-aligned textured quad with default `[0,1]` UVs into the shared 2D render queue, using the supplied texture, authored rectangle, tint, and render flags.

functions.name: queue_axis_aligned_textured_quad_uv
functions.description: Appends one axis-aligned textured quad with explicit UV bounds into the shared 2D render queue. Front-end widgets, progress bars, and the damage gauge all use this helper.

functions.name: queue_textured_quad_corners
functions.description: Appends one arbitrary four-corner textured quad with explicit per-corner positions and UV bounds into the shared 2D render queue. The backdrop and galaxy-line renderers use this helper.

functions.name: layout_and_queue_wrapped_font_text
functions.description: Splits one text string into wrapped line runs, queues each run through `queue_font_text_instance`, and returns the aligned authored-space origin and measured bounds for the wrapped block.

functions.name: initialize_font3d_objects
functions.description: Void materializer for the registered font atlas: borrows one ObjectList slot per glyph into the fixed 128-entry g_font3d_bods array, applies the glyph UVs and x scale, and records the matching scale in g_font3d_scales.

functions.name: query_directx_runtime_version
functions.description: Dynamically probes DirectDraw, DirectInput, and Direct3D 8 availability and returns the highest DirectX-style runtime version code reached by the startup capability check.

functions.name: append_enumerated_input_controller_callback
functions.description: DirectInput device-enumeration callback that creates one joystick device from the reported instance, appends it to the global controller list, and stops enumeration once four devices are present.

functions.name: configure_input_controller_axis_range_callback
functions.description: DirectInput object-enumeration callback that applies the native `[-1000, 1000]` range property to each axis object on one enumerated input controller.

functions.name: update_joystick_input
functions.description: Polls each enumerated DirectInput joystick device, reacquires on transient loss, normalizes its primary X/Y axes into the shared [-1000, 1000] range, packs the sampled buttons into the game's bitmask format, and publishes the result through the input-controller slot writer.

functions.name: release_input_controllers
functions.description: Releases every enumerated DirectInput controller device plus the shared DirectInput interface and clears the global controller list.

functions.name: initialize_keyboard_input
functions.description: Initializes the DirectInput keyboard device, sets its data format/cooperative level, acquires it, and clears the previous/current key-state buffers.

functions.name: update_keyboard_input
functions.description: Polls the DirectInput keyboard device into the current and previous 0x100-byte key-state tables, retries reacquisition on transient failures, and publishes the derived digital input flags and directional axes into the shared input-controller lanes.

functions.name: is_key_pressed_edge
functions.description: Returns true when the supplied keyboard scancode is down in the current DirectInput key-state table and was up in the previous frame's table.

functions.name: is_key_down
functions.description: Returns true when the supplied keyboard scancode is down in the current DirectInput key-state table.

functions.name: release_keyboard_input
functions.description: Releases the DirectInput keyboard device and its shared DirectInput interface, clearing the global keyboard-input handles.

functions.name: initialize_mouse_authored_scale_from_clip_rect
functions.description: Caches the current clipped screen rectangle and initializes the shared mouse screen-to-authored scaling for the default 640x480 authored coordinate space.

functions.name: update_mouse_authored_scale
functions.description: Computes the shared mouse screen-to-authored X/Y scale factors from the cached clip rectangle and the supplied authored width and height. convert_mouse_screen_xy consumes those scales.

functions.name: resolve_uncaptured_cursor_sensitivity_scale
functions.description: Returns the cursor-delta sensitivity scale for uncaptured pointer mode: 1.0 while the cursor is captured, otherwise arg * 1.8 + 0.7.

functions.name: update_mouse
functions.description: Polls the DirectInput mouse through a 20-byte DIMOUSESTATE2-compatible owner, accumulates and clamps live pointer coordinates, selects captured or uncaptured Win32 clip rectangles, and forwards wheel/button state into InputControllerSlot 0.

functions.name: set_hide_system_cursor_flag
functions.description: Sets the shared latch that tells `update_mouse` whether to hide the OS cursor after the current pointer-region update.

functions.name: click_mouse_screen
functions.description: Optionally forwards a cursor position to SetCursorPos when the window is active and captured, then stores raw/live mouse coordinates for the selected slot and publishes authored x/y through player zero's borrowed cRGameInput owner.

functions.name: convert_mouse_screen_xy
functions.description: Side-effect-only Windows cursor bridge: copies a captured OS cursor directly or converts centered cursor deltas into authored x/y, applies steering sensitivity only while uncaptured, zeros both outputs on query failure, and recenters the active main window. Native leaves incidental eax values; the helper has a void ABI.

functions.name: release_mouse_input
functions.description: Releases the DirectInput mouse device and its shared DirectInput interface, clearing the global mouse-input handles.

functions.name: is_mouse_captured
functions.description: Returns the active byte at the head of the player-owned cRMouse-compatible subobject stored at GameRoot +0x290; iOS names the same method cRMouse::IsActive().

functions.name: capture_mouse_cursor
functions.description: Implements the Windows cRMouse::SetActive() lane at GameRoot +0x290: snapshots live x/y, warps controller zero through click_mouse_screen, marks the one-frame draw suppression latch, and enables the active byte.

functions.name: release_mouse_cursor
functions.description: Implements cRMouse::SetInActive() for the player-owned mouse subobject by restoring saved x/y into the live lanes when active and clearing byte zero.

functions.name: load_object_definition
functions.description: Implements the staticized void `cRObject::Load(char*)` path: parses one authored object definition into the caller-owned Object slot, installs its owned vertex and facequad arrays, and borrows texture refs from the shared manager.

functions.name: initialize_global_identity_matrix_thunk
functions.description: VC6 local `_$E2` CRT registration thunk emitted by the authored `g_identity_matrix(1.0f)` definition; it tail-forwards to the adjacent local dynamic initializer.

functions.name: initialize_global_identity_matrix
functions.description: VC6 local `_$E1` dynamic initializer emitted by the authored `g_identity_matrix(1.0f)` definition. It directly calls the single-float matrix constructor and is only reached through the CRT registration thunk.

functions.name: convert_math_type32_to_16
functions.description: Converts one float into the shared signed-16 gameplay math representation by scaling with `65536 / scale` and truncating. Android symbols match this helper to `MathType32to16(float, float)`, and both Android and Windows only xref it from the SubGoldy update path.

functions.name: convert_math_type16_to_32
functions.description: Converts one signed-16 gameplay math value back into float form by scaling with `scale / 65536`. Android symbols match this helper to `MathType16to32(short, float)`, and both Android and Windows only xref it from the SubGoldy update path.

functions.name: initialize_math_random_table
functions.description: Fills the shared 0x1fff-entry gameplay random table from the underlying linear-congruential generator and resets the ring index. Android symbols match this helper family to `gRMathRand2Init()`.

functions.name: next_math_random_value
functions.description: Advances the shared gameplay random-table index modulo 0x1fff and returns the next cached 15-bit value. Android symbols match this helper family to the shared `Rand()` / `gRMathRand2()` accessors.

functions.name: j_rand
functions.description: Game-code jump thunk to the statically linked CRT rand body, shared by the signed and unsigned random-float scaling helpers.

functions.name: initialize_trigonometry_tables
functions.description: Builds the shared cosine and sine lookup tables used by the gameplay math wrappers during startup, then resets the companion random table state.

functions.name: set_math_random_seed
functions.description: Stores one caller-provided seed into the shared gameplay linear-congruential generator before the random table is consumed. Android symbols match this helper family to `RandSeed(int)`.

functions.name: cosine
functions.description: Computes one cosine value through the shared gameplay trigonometry wrapper. Android symbols match this helper to `Cos(float)`.

functions.name: sine
functions.description: Computes one sine value through the shared gameplay trigonometry wrapper. Android symbols match this helper to `Sin(float)`.

functions.name: arccosine
functions.description: Computes the arccosine of one float input. Android symbols match this helper to `ACos(float)`.

functions.name: atan2_positive
functions.description: Computes a two-argument arctangent with the result wrapped into the positive full-turn range. Android symbols match this helper to `ATan(float, float)`.

functions.name: square_root
functions.description: Computes the square root of one float input. Android symbols match this helper to `Sqrt(float)`.

functions.name: multiply_vector_by_matrix_copy
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the exact value-returning `Vector3::operator*(const TransformMatrix&) const`. Its hidden output pointer exposes the ordinary VC6 aggregate-return ABI. Android and iOS retain `tVector::operator*(const tMatrix&) const`.

functions.name: dot_vectors
functions.description: Exact static `Vector3::dot_vectors(const Vector3&, const Vector3&)` overload: computes the 3D dot product of two const-reference operands and returns it as double, matching Android `tVector::Dot(const tVector&, const tVector&)`.

functions.name: dot_vector
functions.description: Exact float-returning `Vector3::dot_vector(const Vector3&)` member: computes the 3D dot product against one const-reference operand, matching iOS and Android `tVector::Dot(const tVector&)`.

functions.name: multiply_vector_by_matrix
functions.description: Exact void by-value `Vector3::operator*=(TransformMatrix)` retained by iOS as `tVector::operator*=(tMatrix)`. It applies the affine basis and translation in place; Android exposes the analogous operation as `tVector::Multiply(const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: rotate_vector_by_matrix
functions.description: Exact reference-returning `Vector3::rotate_vector_by_matrix(const TransformMatrix&)` harness for Android/iOS `tVector::Rotate(const tMatrix&)`. It applies only the 3x3 rotational basis in place; sprite-facing callers consume the returned receiver.

functions.name: normalize_vector
functions.description: Exact float-returning `Vector3::normalize_vector()` member: normalizes its receiver in place and returns the prior magnitude, matching iOS and Android `tVector::Normalize()`.

functions.name: vector_magnitude
functions.description: Exact const `Vector3::vector_magnitude()` member: returns sqrt(x*x + y*y + z*z) as float without mutating its receiver, matching iOS and Android `tVector::Magnitude() const`.

functions.name: normalize_vector_from_source
functions.description: Exact float-returning `Vector3::normalize_vector_from_source(const Vector3&)` member: copies a const-reference source into its receiver, normalizes it, and returns the prior magnitude. Android retains `tVector::Normalize(const tVector&)`.

functions.name: cross_vectors
functions.description: Exact void `Vector3::cross_vectors(const Vector3&, const Vector3&)` member: computes the 3D cross product of two const-reference operands through a function-local static temporary and copies it into its receiver, matching iOS and Android `tVector::Cross(const tVector&, const tVector&)`.

functions.name: destroy_cross_vectors_static_result
functions.description: Compiler-generated empty destruction callback registered for cross_vectors' function-local static Vector3 result.

functions.name: initialize_uniform_scale_matrix
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the authored single-float `tMatrix::tMatrix(float)` constructor retained by Android. The exact constructor writes the scalar across the xyz diagonal, zeroes off-diagonal and translation slots, and sets homogeneous `w` to 1.0.

functions.name: rotate_matrix_world_x
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the exact void `TransformMatrix::rotate_matrix_local_x(float)` member, retained by iOS and Android as `tMatrix::RotLocalX(float)`. It rotates the up/forward basis around the matrix's local X axis.

functions.name: rotate_matrix_world_y
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the exact void `TransformMatrix::rotate_matrix_local_y(float)` member, retained by iOS and Android as `tMatrix::RotLocalY(float)`. It rotates the right/forward basis around the matrix's local Y axis.

functions.name: rotate_matrix_world_z
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the exact void `TransformMatrix::rotate_matrix_local_z(float)` member, retained by iOS and Android as `tMatrix::RotLocalZ(float)`. It rotates the right/up basis around the matrix's local Z axis.

functions.name: initialize_matrix_from_values
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the authored 16-float `tMatrix::tMatrix(float, ..., float)` constructor retained by Android and iOS. The exact constructor writes all four matrix rows and returns its receiver only through the ordinary VC6 constructor ABI.

functions.name: multiply_matrices
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::multiply_matrices(const TransformMatrix&, const TransformMatrix&)` member: writes `this = lhs * rhs` for the 3x3 basis and translated origin row. Android retains `tMatrix::Multiply(const tMatrix&, const tMatrix&)`; the right operand left in EAX is incidental.

functions.name: multiply_matrix_in_place
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the exact void `TransformMatrix::multiply_matrix(const TransformMatrix&)` member retained by iOS and Android as `tMatrix::Multiply(const tMatrix&)`. It snapshots its receiver and delegates to the two-reference multiply core.

functions.name: multiply_matrix_in_place_forward_thunk
functions.description: Exact void thiscall forwarder that passes one const-reference rhs matrix to the owned `TransformMatrix::multiply_matrix` member. Its source-level operator identity remains deliberately unresolved.

functions.name: premultiply_matrix_in_place
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::premultiply_matrix_in_place(const TransformMatrix&)` member: snapshots its receiver and writes `this = lhs * saved_this`. The core's incidental EAX points at the dead stack snapshot, proving no meaningful return contract.

functions.name: set_matrix_identity
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::set_matrix_identity()` method: resets the complete 4x4 transform including translation, matching mobile `tMatrix::Identity()`.

functions.name: set_matrix_rotation_identity
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::set_matrix_rotation_identity()` method: resets the rotational basis and homogeneous row while preserving translation, matching mobile `tMatrix::RotIdentity()`.

functions.name: invert_matrix_in_place
functions.description: Void `tMatrix::Invert()` implementation: inverts one orthonormal transform matrix in place by transposing the basis and recomputing the negated translated origin. Windows and Android callers discard the incidental receiver register.

functions.name: invert_matrix_from_source
functions.description: Void `tMatrix::Invert(tMatrix const&)` implementation: writes the inverse of a source orthonormal transform into a destination matrix. Its ordinary callers discard the result register, while cRPlayer/cROverlay AI only tail-call it under their independently void callback contracts.

functions.name: orthogonalize_matrix
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::orthogonalize_matrix()` method: normalizes the three basis vectors and rebuilds an orthogonal frame through cross products, matching mobile `tMatrix::Orthoganalize()`.

functions.name: set_matrix_z_direction
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::set_matrix_z_direction(const Vector3&)` method: normalizes a requested forward direction and rebuilds the right/up basis, matching mobile `tMatrix::SetZDir(tVector const&)`.

functions.name: look_at_point
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::look_at_point(const Vector3&)` method: subtracts the matrix position from a world-space target and forwards the resulting direction to SetZDir, matching mobile `tMatrix::LookAt(tVector const&)`.

functions.name: initialize_quaternion_from_axis
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the exact void `Quaternion::operator=(const AxisAngle&)` conversion. It applies `sin(angle / 2)` to xyz and `cos(angle / 2)` to w. Android exposes the analogous math as `tQuaternian::tQuaternian(const tAxis&)`, but the Windows body does not establish the receiver return required by a constructor.

functions.name: initialize_axis_from_quaternion
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the exact void `AxisAngle::operator=(const Quaternion&)` conversion retained by Android as `tAxis::operator=(const tQuaternian&)`. It normalizes xyz against `sin(theta / 2)` and stores `angle = 2 * acos(w)`.

functions.name: initialize_quaternion_from_matrix
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the authored `tQuaternian::tQuaternian(const tMatrix&)` constructor retained by Android. The typed const-reference constructor builds a quaternion from the matrix's rotational basis.

functions.name: initialize_matrix_from_quaternion
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the authored `tMatrix::tMatrix(const tQuaternian&)` constructor retained by Android. The exact typed const-reference constructor builds the rotation basis and resets translation and homogeneous slots to identity values.

functions.name: interpolate_matrix_rotation
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::interpolate_matrix_rotation(float)` implementation of the authored mobile `tMatrix::Interpolate(float)` member. It extracts and copy-assigns the current rotation as a quaternion, scales its axis angle, and rebuilds the matrix when the rotation is nonzero.

functions.name: linear_interpolate_matrix
functions.description: Exact void `TransformMatrix::linear_interpolate_matrix(const TransformMatrix&, const TransformMatrix&, float)` method: blends rotation in matrix space and translation linearly, matching mobile `tMatrix::LinearInterpolate(tMatrix const&, tMatrix const&, float)`.

functions.name: noop_this_constructor
functions.description: Exact three-byte identity body shared by compiler-folded trivial default constructors. Android independently retains trivial `tColour::tColour()` and `tColourSmall::tColourSmall()` bodies, while the 51 Windows xrefs also span axis-angle/quaternion temporaries, callback objects, and startup globals; the folded address therefore establishes several real aliases but no unique owner.

functions.name: set_color_rgba
functions.description: Exact Windows `tColour::Set(float, float, float, float)` role: stores the four RGBA floats at `+0/+4/+8/+12` and returns the same destination pointer. Android preserves the class, method name, field order, and four stores.

functions.name: set_color_alpha
functions.description: Exact void `tColour::Alpha(float)` role: stores one float at the alpha lane `+0x0c`. Both Windows callers discard EAX, and the honest float/void source remains exact at 3/3 instructions without a synthetic integer return.

functions.name: set_color_grayscale
functions.description: Exact void `tColour::Grey(float)` role: stores one uniform RGB value and forces alpha to `1.0`. Its callers treat it as a write-only method; the value left in EAX is incidental.

functions.name: store_color4f
functions.description: Stable Windows harness identity for the authored `tColour::tColour(float, float, float, float)` constructor role. It stores RGBA at `+0/+4/+8/+12`; all callers treat it as write-only, so the final component register left live by the epilogue is not a return value.

functions.name: set_color_rgb
functions.description: Exact void `tColour::Set(float, float, float)` role: stores RGB at `+0/+4/+8` while preserving the existing alpha lane. Android retains the same overload.

functions.name: pack_color_rgba_u8
functions.description: Exact Windows implementation of the authored `tColourSmall::operator=(tColour const&)` role: converts one four-float RGBA tuple in `[0,1]` into the four-byte BGRA record used by sprite and mesh draw paths. Android independently preserves the same `+2/+1/+0/+3` stores.

functions.name: set_color_white
functions.description: Exact void `tColour::White()` role: tail-calls the recovered Grey helper with `1.0f`, producing opaque white. Android retains the same method.

functions.name: set_color_black
functions.description: Exact void `tColour::Black()` role: tail-calls the recovered Grey helper with `0.0f`, producing opaque black. Android retains the same method.

functions.name: random_signed_float_below
functions.description: Returns a centered random float in `[-scale, scale)` by shifting the engine RNG around zero before scaling by the supplied magnitude.

functions.name: random_float_below
functions.description: Returns a uniform random float in `[0, upper_bound)` by scaling the engine RNG output with the supplied upper bound.

functions.name: initialize_sound_bank
functions.description: Exact Windows `cRSound::Init(cRSoundBank*)` member: registers the 51 shipped `g_sound_bank_entries` records, stores each returned sample id at record `+0x04`, and stops at the 52nd record whose path points at an empty string. Android names the corresponding global `gSFXBank` and preserves the same 12-byte record layout.

functions.name: play_sound_effect_at_position
functions.description: Windows cRSound positional overload: folds a one-listener minimum-distance loop over GamePlayer[0]'s owned camera transform position, then plays one effect id with 25-unit attenuation and left-right pan derived from the supplied Vector3 reference.

functions.name: play_sound_effect
functions.description: Exact Windows `cRSound::Play(int)` member: forwards one effect id into the shared audio backend with the default volume, pitch, and pan parameters.

functions.name: play_sound_effect_scaled
functions.description: Exact Windows `cRSound::PlayVolume(int, float)` member: forwards one effect id and explicit gain into the shared audio backend, leaving pitch and pan on their default lanes.

functions.name: play_warning_sample_backend
functions.description: Exact SoundEffectManager warning wrapper that plays one sample id through the shared registered-sample backend and returns the live sample handle.

functions.name: stop_warning_sample_handle
functions.description: Exact SoundEffectManager warning wrapper that stops one live warning-sample handle through the shared registered-sample backend.

functions.name: initialize_global_sprite_pool_colors_thunk
functions.description: CRT constructor-table thunk that tail-forwards to the SpriteManager pool color-subobject initializer.

functions.name: initialize_global_sprite_pool_colors
functions.description: Runs the folded trivial tColour constructor over the +0x2c color member of all 3000 sprites embedded in the global SpriteManager at a 0xb4-byte stride.

functions.name: initialize_global_sprite_sentinel_color_thunk
functions.description: CRT constructor-table thunk that tail-forwards to initialization of the global sentinel Sprite's color subobject.

functions.name: initialize_global_sprite_sentinel_color
functions.description: Runs the folded trivial tColour constructor for the +0x2c color member of the standalone global sprite-allocation sentinel.

functions.name: initialize_sprite
functions.description: Initializes one sprite slot in the `cRSprite` runtime family before manager allocation links it into active/free lists.

functions.name: update_sprite
functions.description: Runs one live sprite's per-frame state update; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSprite::AI()`.

functions.name: register_sprite_texture
functions.description: Registers or reuses one sprite texture reference; iOS RSprite.o names this manager family `cRSpriteManager::Load(char*, int, int)` and also exposes the adjacent `LoadSet` variant.

functions.name: initialize_sprite_manager
functions.description: Initializes the global sprite manager, its sprite pool, and free-list heads; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::Init()`.

functions.name: kill_sprite
functions.description: Unlinks and returns one live sprite to the manager free list; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSprite::Kill()`.

functions.name: allocate_sprite
functions.description: Allocates one sprite from the manager free list, assigns texture ids, and links it into the selected active bucket; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::New(int, int, int, int)`.

functions.name: kill_game_sprites
functions.description: Kills all nonpersistent gameplay sprites while preserving manager state; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::KillGame()`.

functions.name: update_sprite_facing_angle
functions.description: Refreshes the sprite angle lane at `+0x7c` from motion delta in camera space, with the optional smoothing timer carried in `+0x8c/+0x90`. iOS RSprite.o names the matrix-driven tail/facing family `cRSprite::BuildTail(tMatrix*)`.

functions.name: set_sprite_manager_paused
functions.description: Writes the shared sprite-manager pause byte consumed by `update_sprite`, freezing or unfreezing sprite updates globally. iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::Pause(bool)`.

functions.name: set_sprite_texture_ref
functions.description: Assigns one sprite's texture reference and frame metadata from a manager texture id; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSprite::SetTextureRef(int, int)`.

functions.name: get_sprite_texture
functions.description: Returns the texture object for a manager texture id; iOS RSprite.o names this `cRSpriteManager::GetTexture(int)`.

functions.name: get_sprite_texture_ref
functions.description: Returns the borrowed TGA header and inline pixel payload at `TextureRef::texture_ref +0x98` for one manager texture id. iOS and Android distinguish this as `cRSpriteManager::GetTga(int)`; their separate `GetTextureRef(int)` accessor instead returns record `+0x8c`.

functions.name: ascii_upper_if_lowercase
functions.description: Exact Windows `RstrASC(char)`: folds one lowercase ASCII byte to uppercase and leaves all other byte values unchanged.

functions.name: rstrcpy_checked_ascii
functions.description: Exact Windows `Rstrcpy(char*, char const*)`: copies a null-terminated resource string while the Windows build additionally reports high-bit bytes through its `Dodgy Rstrcpy` diagnostic. This helper is hot across path, text, and script setup code.

functions.name: find_case_insensitive_substring
functions.description: Windows `Rstrfind(char*, char*)`: scans the second argument for the first ASCII-case-insensitive match of the first argument and returns that searched-string cursor. Used for prefix-friendly parser metadata and tag probes.

functions.name: advance_to_next_crlf_line
functions.description: Exact Windows `Rstrnewline(char*)`: returns the first byte after the next CRLF pair, or null when there is no following non-empty line.

functions.name: strings_equal_case_insensitive_path
functions.description: ASCII case-insensitive equality comparator used by path, mesh, landscape, and music lookup helpers. Unlike the earlier parser comparator, both strings must terminate after the folded match.

functions.name: parse_next_signed_int
functions.description: Exact Windows `Rstrint(char**)`: advances a caller-owned text cursor to the next numeric token, parses an optional sign and decimal digits, and leaves the cursor after the consumed integer. Shared by shipped asset and level-script parsers.

functions.name: sample_tga_pixel_rgb
functions.description: Samples one bottom-up TGA-style pixel from the loaded font texture sheet buffer, expanding 8-bit grayscale pixels or returning packed 24-bit RGB.

functions.name: get_or_create_texture_ref
functions.description: Looks up or allocates one TextureRefList entry, using the 0xa4-byte TextureRef stride, 0x80-byte inline path, no-reuse flag, and slot-index/count bookkeeping behind sprite texture registration.

functions.name: attach_render_camera_source
functions.description: Attaches a source render object to one runtime camera/render slot and marks the slot active through flag bit 0.

functions.name: initialize_render_camera_slot
functions.description: Initializes one 0x28-byte runtime camera/render slot used by render_game_frame, seeding viewport lanes, draw-world state, and the default 0.8725f lane while leaving owner fields for later setup.

functions.name: initialize_translation_matrix
functions.description: DirectX 8 SDK `D3DXMatrixTranslation` static-library body built with VC7 build 9178. Retained as a semantic and extent reference for game callsites, but excluded from authored gameplay matching totals.

functions.name: build_perspective_projection_matrix
functions.description: DirectX 8 SDK `D3DXMatrixPerspectiveFovRH` static-library body built with VC7 build 9178. Retained as a semantic and extent reference for `render_camera` and toon-outline callsites, but excluded from authored gameplay matching totals.

functions.name: build_camera_view_matrix
functions.description: DirectX 8 SDK `D3DXMatrixLookAtRH` static-library body built with VC7 build 9178. Retained as a semantic and extent reference for `render_camera`, but excluded from authored gameplay matching totals.
